calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: SD-033b/MECH-263 OFC outcome-devaluation behavioural retest on the DECOUPLED devaluation head (supersedes 485l)
items.note: Clears the re-derive brake fired by failure_autopsy_V3-EXQ-485l (10th non_contributory autopsy on SD-033b/MECH-263): the named upstream substrate -- the OFC devaluation-head clamp DECOUPLE (OFCConfig.use_devaluation_head / compute_devaluation_bias, +/-ofc_devaluation_bias_scale, default 2.0) -- is now BUILT (ree-v3 main 758956f, ree-v3/CLAUDE.md 'SD-033b GAP-8 DECOUPLE'), so this re-test is meaningful (decoupled-head structural redesign on built substrate, NOT another letter circling the shared-clamp ceiling, NOT the refused plain 485m gain-tweak). Drives+reads the devalued re-ranking + MECH-449 No-Go viability from the SEPARATE devaluation_bias_head (own larger clamp); C2 discrimination stays on state_bias_head (+/-0.5, untraded); readiness gate (b) re-stated on the decoupled-head devalued range. claim_ids=[SD-033b, MECH-263]; PROMOTES NOTHING until it scores. Smoke: end-to-end dry-run on the ree-cloud-1 (linux-x86_64-py3.10) class PASS (FAIL/substrate_not_ready_requeue self-route correct at dry scale; nogo_applied=1, excluded=5, C3 silence 1/1). Pinned ree-cloud-4 (Mac at load 167; heavy phased-training run).
